name: Parsl On Slurm
summary: Run small Parsl workflows on Slurm with explicit provider and executor limits.
description: This skill helps Python workflow authors configure Parsl with SlurmProvider and HighThroughputExecutor while keeping blocks, worker counts, walltime, account, partition, logs, and smoke-test behavior explicit.

inputs.name: account
inputs.description: Site-specific Slurm account or allocation for Parsl worker blocks.

inputs.name: partition
inputs.description: Slurm partition or queue for Parsl worker blocks.

inputs.name: block_shape
inputs.description: Nodes per block, init/min/max blocks, cores per node, workers per node, walltime, and optional scheduler directives.

inputs.name: python_environment
inputs.description: Optional virtualenv or Conda/Mamba environment containing Parsl.

outputs.name: parsl_run_dir
outputs.description: Parsl run directory with provider, executor, worker, and task logs.

outputs.name: parsl_plan
outputs.description: JSON launch plan written in dry-run and run modes.

outputs.name: parsl_result
outputs.description: Small JSON result report from the Parsl smoke workflow when RUN_PARSL=1.

examples.title: Dry-run or launch a Parsl Slurm demo

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Run the wrapper with RUN_PARSL=0 to inspect the launch plan, then set RUN_PARSL=1 with one small block on a test partition and confirm the result JSON is written.
